title: Expose graph availability in snapshots
goal: Surface graph availability/staleness/profile-exclusion states and ensure stale graph does not influence retrieval

acceptance.assertion: The slice is implemented or explicitly closed against current main/PR state with no hidden broadening.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

acceptance.assertion: RepoBrief boundaries are preserved: no hidden refresh, no Git mutation in RepoBrief core, no review/runtime correctness claim.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

acceptance.assertion: Relevant tests/checks are run, or a precise blocker/gap is recorded.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

acceptance.assertion: The output states what the evidence does not establish.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

metadata.outcome: Implemented and verified graph availability projection in Lenskit snapshot availability model.
validation: Lenskit PR #903 CI green including pytest-full, ruff, doc-freshness, task-index and CodeQL

verified_acceptance.rbv1-t015-scope-bound: Lenskit PR #903 adds graph_availability to snapshot_availability_model only; no hidden broadening into graph production or refresh.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t015-boundary-preserved: The projection is diagnostic/read-only; it does not build graphs, mutate Git, refresh snapshots or claim runtime/review correctness.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t015-tests-or-gap: Targeted local tests passed and PR #903 CI was green.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t015-non-claims: Proof and graph_availability does_not_establish preserve graph completeness, dependency completeness, test sufficiency, runtime behavior, review impact, retrieval improvement and merge readiness.
